# Transcrição: 03-Communicating to Convince and Convert

- **Fonte:** 03-Communicating to Convince and Convert
- **Duração:** 07:27
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:05]

Think back to the last conversation that you had that went beyond how are you. Maybe it was with your neighbor or family member or a barista at your favorite coffee shop because they gave you the wrong drink. What was your goal? I guarantee there was an outcome you wanted. For your neighbor to watch your dog next time you're out of town. For your child to do their homework before they get online. For the barista to make you a new drink. Here's my point. In every interaction of substance, we have a desired outcome. And that goes for your business too. I'm Anastasia and in this lesson we're unpacking how to communicate to your audience to influence, persuade and inspire them to take action. Whether it's an email, a social media post, a video message. Anytime you're connecting to your audience you'll use the same principles and techniques to capture their attention, speak to their hearts and inspire them to take action. This is about more than just how to write sales pages or craft emails and blogs to only sell your offer. You'll definitely use those principles to do that too. But you're not always selling a product or a service. You communicate to build trust with your audience. To show them that you're the expert, that you can help them solve their greatest challenges, that you deeply understand them, that you've walked in their path, that you're just a few steps ahead. Whenever you communicate, there's something that you want your audience to think, feel or do. This video will lay out the foundation for what makes persuasive communication and will give you questions to ask before writing, recording or publishing anything. Let's get into it. You cannot convince, persuade or influence if you're not connecting with your audience. How you communicate is how you will connect to your audience. Communication is all about connection. It's how you build trust, respect, authority. It's how people come to know you, your story. It's how they learn to trust you, to see themselves in you. Again, you cannot transform someone's life. You cannot make massive impact on the world. You cannot get someone to say yes to your offer if they don't feel connected to you. Communication is all about psychology, about human emotion, which we all understand at some level. We make decisions based on our emotions, fear, desire, pain or pleasure. Think about the last purchase you made. What motivated you to buy? Were you moving toward pleasure, away from pain? What did you hope to gain? More time, more joy, more love, less fear, less insecurity, less sadness, less loneliness, People take action for one of two reasons. They want to move away from pain or fear, and they want to move toward pleasure or something they desire. Communication is never about you. It's always about your audience and what they're experiencing, their hopes and dreams, their

## [03:05 - 06:07]

pain and struggles and longing to experience something more, something different. If you talk about yourself, your program, why they need you, it won't grab their attention. But get them to feel seen, understood. They'll feel connected to you. They'll trust that you can help them on their journey toward pleasure and away from pain. We've all had that friend in our lives who only talks about themselves. You know everything about them, from what toothpaste they use to what they had for lunch. But they never ask about you or connect their stories to what's going on in your life, what you care about. We don't want to engage with those people any more than we have to. Don't be that person. Maya Angelou said, people will forget what you said. People will forget what you did, but people will never forget how you made them feel. Whenever you communicate, your goal must be to make your audience feel or think something that will inspire them to take action. And this action won't always be saying yes to your offer, although sometimes it will. When you inspire them to take action, you could help them embrace a new point of view, see themselves and their world differently. Make them feel understood, maybe for the first time. Or show them a different way to make their lives easier, better and less painful. Now that you understand how to connect, it's time to create. Before writing anything, social media posts, emails, scripts for YouTube, you want to think about the following questions. And they're on your worksheet too. So be sure to write down your answers. This will help you gain clarity. Question one. Who am I communicating to? This is your ideal audience. Remember, you're not writing for everybody, only the people that you're trying to serve. Picture this person. What are their pains, pleasures? What are they fearful about? Question two. What value am I trying to provide to the person who will consume my message? This might be their big challenge, the pain that they're experiencing in their lives. It might be the thing that they desire the most. Or it might just be a small sliver of hope, entertainment, encouragement. And you don't have to give it to them all at once. One small piece, one small step forward is how you create relationships and make progress. Question three. How am I solving it? This is the solution to their problem that you've identified or stepped toward their goals and dreams. It might be information, a story, encouragement, a tool, even a meme with a quote is a solution. The goal of your content is to help them move toward pleasure and away from pain. Question four. How will the solution benefit them? And be as specific as possible. This won't always go into what you're writing or posting, but you should know how their life will be better as a result of them consuming your message. Again, there must be a benefit. And one final tip. Is there anything happening in the world or something that's top of mind for people that

## [06:07 - 07:27]

can impact their frame of reference or take their attention? In our world, there's a lot going on and we need to be aware of what the larger community is thinking about. For example, is it a holiday? Are there any newsworthy events that will make you sound tone deaf if you post without acknowledgement? Is your audience made up of parents who are dealing with back to school season or summer vacations? Know what's going on in your client's minds, hearts, and in their world so you can deeply understand where they're at. So here's where people get this wrong. Especially early on, we become so proud of our program, our course, our mastermind, that we want to talk about it. We want to talk about ourselves, why the program is so great. This won't grab someone's attention unless you link it to them. Alternatively, we don't want to avoid talking about our offers. We just need to frame it right. See people care about how you can help them. But the benefits and outcomes they'll get to through their connection to you and how you add value. Remember, it's all about emotion. Making your ideal audience feel seen, heard, cared for and that you can help them move away from pain and closer to pleasure. When you use this approach, you're going to make them feel. So they'll be inspired to take action.
